description: §61.1 — an <endpoint> arm body that references an undefined identifier (<Ping : { ok: true, val: nonexistentVar }>) SHALL fire E-SCOPE-001, exactly as the same undefined var does in a normal logic expression. Regression guard: the arm body was lowered from a raw string by emit-server and never scope-checked, so nonexistentVar compiled clean and emitted a FREE variable into the server route-handler (runtime ReferenceError) — a fail-open that contradicts §61.1's inbound-honesty guarantee. The valid <Dispatch(prompt)> arm (payload binding) stays clean.
rationale: §61.1: because scrml owns the inbound decode + dispatch, an <endpoint> arm body is compiler-owned honest code — an undeclared identifier is a hole the compiler can see, so it SHALL be loud at compile time (E-SCOPE-001), not a silent runtime ReferenceError. The arm's own positional payload bindings (prompt) and module fns / globals remain in scope.
